entry.title: Re: How to find the logic where the particular value is changed for dynamic variable in debugger.
entry.content:
Very interesting question.
As far as I know there is not really an easy way for this, if there is I would also like to know.

How I tend to do this (had a situation recently):

  •  Put a break where you have a view on the field
  • Go to the stack trace and look back step by step to follow everything that happens with this field
    In a sense look at the flow backwards
  • If required set breakpoints on earlier detected places with the stack trace and restart the flow

In my case this led to a finding whereby standard coding made a IF ELSE that impacted the field in question much deeper in the stack and that way I could see why it took ELSE over IF and found an answer and solution.
Of course this is not always possible, for those flows where it works with global objects it gets more difficult and then I basically set a breakpoint on every where-used finding of the object that looks like a potential interesting spot.

entry.title: Re: SAP Build App request destination 401
entry.content:
@lulu4 

Destination you have entered is in correct for properties "api" .. pls correct

entry.title: Re: Build Custom Journal Entry Posting Service Based On I_JournalEntryTP By Creating Own RAP Object
entry.content:
Hi @TakeZhou ,

Thank you for this excellent demo. I have another use case that is currently driving me to despair. Mybe you can give me a hint.

I have created an action in my BO that allows me to retrieve data from a third-party system. I save the data using managed create. The user is now supposed to check the data. They can make a test posting using a button. Then they should make the correct posting using another button.

Since I am no longer in create phase, I am unable to call up the I_JournalEntryTP~Post directly. I have now tried this via application job. It dumps in the action. But it can't be right that I have to save a dummy value via modify in order to get into the save sequence, can it? 

What do you think?

entry.title: SAP Integrated Business Planning (SAP IBP) 2602 - Available Now!
entry.content:
SAP Integrated Business Planning 2602 is now generally available, and the new year starts with great news: Harmonized scenario management is finally here!

Harmonized scenario management is a new capability that unifies and simplifies how you work with scenarios across orders and key figures in SAP IBP. With harmonized scenarios you manage the complete scenario lifecycle in the Planner Workspaces app: You can create, modify, and promote both order and key figure data within a single scenario framework.

Key improvements include the ability to run simulations on top of scenarios for orders, extended scenario lifetimes that cover both orders and key figures, and new promotion options, including promotion for orders.

Harmonized scenario management offers controlled shine-through behavior that now includes orders, giving you better control of how baseline changes flow through your planning environment.

Alongside the harmonized planning area, this enhancement takes integrated planning in SAP IBP to the next level, letting orders and forecasts work together seamlessly without requiring you to manage separate processes.

 

 

Now let’s explore some of the other highlights of the first SAP IBP release in 2026:

 

Supply

Cut Waste, Boost Accuracy: Shelf Life Planning with Characteristics-Based Planning Attributes

We now combine shelf life planning with characteristics-based planning (CBP) attributes to strengthen inventory management, improve planning accuracy, and ensure delivery of products within their usable life, reducing waste and boosting customer satisfaction. Effective shelf life management also supports regulatory compliance and minimizes financial losses from expired inventory.

The new capabilities let you define shelf life for products based on specific characteristics, take expiration dates of planning elements (such as stock) into account, and select the correct batch by evaluating both product characteristics and shelf life.

 

 

Automate End-to-End Distribution with Intelligent Deployment Conversion

Our new deployment conversion run algorithm automatically converts stock transfer requisitions (STRs) into deployment requisitions. Unlike order-based supply planning runs that create STRs, the deployment conversion run only uses a subset of supply types that is configured as available to deploy, such as available stock and scheduled inbound deliveries.

The powerful algorithm leverages your existing supply plan to assign available-to-deploy supplies across your network, making data-driven deployment decisions that align with your business priorities. A comprehensive set of configurable parameters allows you to customize the deployment logic to match your unique operational requirements.

The system converts STRs based on requirement date priority, processing them systematically from left to right to ensure the most urgent needs are addressed first. You can limit both demand and supply horizons for deployment conversion, allowing you to focus on specific timeframes.

The algorithm considers backward offset adjustments for both demand and supply elements that fall in the past, ensuring comprehensive coverage of your planning requirements. Fixed STRs are automatically included within the deployment conversion scope, providing complete visibility across your distribution network.

When partial available-to-deploy supply situations occur, the system intelligently splits STRs while respecting your specified rounding values.

 

 

Maintenance Repair and Operations (MRO)

Instant AI Analysis of MRO Inventory Runs 

The new AI-Assisted MRO Inventory Analysis delivers a concise summary and intelligent analysis of MRO inventory planning runs, accompanied by a table that highlights the inputs and settings considered. Planners save time by having the data and all nuances involved in inventory planning available in one action. ​

This capability improves the understanding of planning results, increases adoption of planning parameters, and boosts the overall effectiveness of the operator. The one-click AI analysis is available directly in both Planner Workspaces and in the SAP IBP, add-in for Microsoft Excel, and you can review operator settings and configuration on the same screen where planning occurs.

 

 

Planning UIs

Seamless Approval Workflows in Planner Workspaces

New seamless approval workflows let you sign off plans directly in the Planner Workspaces app while viewing the full context needed to make fast, informed decisions. By keeping approvals where the supply chain planning data lives, planners and decision makers save time and avoid switching between separate systems, consolidating tasks into a single, more efficient workspace.

This capability supports formal approval points in complex processes like Demand Planning, ensuring alignment, accountability, and clear management sign-off before moving to the next step. By leveraging SAP Build Process Automation, gated process steps can be embedded within SAP IBP, using out-of-the-box workflow templates to control S&OP approvals.

Users can approve or reject tasks directly in Planner Workspaces and associate planning workbooks, filters, and versions with the workflows used to approve the start or completion of a process step.

For example, during a demand review, a demand planning manager can formally sign off the demand plan while viewing the appropriate aggregated demand data in Planner Workspaces.

 

 

Precise and Flexible Filtering with Advanced Operators

The advanced filtering capabilities that were recently added to the SAP IBP, add-in for Microsoft Excel and the Planning Filters app are now also available in Planner Workspaces, giving you consistent, powerful filtering across your planning UIs.

Advanced operators like "starts with", "contains", and "greater than" enable you to quickly pinpoint exactly the data you need.

Take product searches, for example: When you filter for descriptions containing "1.5 Liters" any new products matching this criterion will automatically appear in your results - no manual filter updates required.

Additional filtering options will be added in upcoming releases.

 

Foundation

Smooth Collaboration with Real-Time Microsoft Teams Messaging

Planners can now send real-time messages to Microsoft Teams channels directly from SAP IBP when saving key figure data or scheduling application jobs, sharing reason codes, comments, and contextual details. By bridging planning actions in SAP IBP with collaboration in Microsoft Teams, this integration reduces communication delays, improves transparency across planning teams, and ensures critical updates instantly reach the right colleagues - including, for example, stakeholders of your S&OP process who aren’t necessarily regular SAP IBP users.

The integration delivers faster collaboration through immediate visibility of planning changes in Microsoft Teams channels, reduced manual effort by removing the need for separate notifications or emails, and stronger accountability by keeping everyone informed about key actions.

Messages can be triggered when saving key figure data in the SAP IBP, add-in for Microsoft Excel or the Planner Workspaces app, or when scheduling application jobs, and you can select the target Microsoft Teams channel directly within SAP IBP.

 

More Information

  • SAP IBP What’s New Webinars 2602
  • 2602 What’s New: Help Portal Documentation
  • What’s New in Best Practices
  • SAP IBP Community Page
  • SAP IBP Roadmap
  • SAP Customer Influence Portal for SAP IBP
  • Questions? Ask here
  • SAP IBP Learning Journey

entry.title: Re: WebSocket RFC – RFC For the Internet
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

please support my Influencing Request:

Add support for Websocket RFC to @sap/cds-rfc and @sap-rfc/node-rfc-library 

Best regards,
Gregor

entry.title: Re: New program: Auth Object Requirements
entry.content:
Hi Sachin, Functional defines “who can do what under which business conditions”.
Explain what users are allowed to do in the program, such as:
- Display / Create / Change / Delete
- Company code, plant, business area, etc.
- Whether approval or sensitive data access is involved

SAP Master Data Governance

entry.title: Re: Unable to send XML file as email from Manage Electron...
entry.content:
Good day @pallavisri 

Thank you for your question.

The error you provided in your screenshot hints at missing or incomplete configuration for sending the source document by email or the recipient email is missing. This issue is addressed in KBA 3251019.

Here is a quick check you can carry out:

  • In your app select the document line, check the Log Icon and see what the detailed message is. The message “could not be sent” is only the wrapper message. The log will tell you whether it is no recipient or you have not configured the email channel. It can also be, you have not defined SOA.
  • Seems your document relates to sales/bill to customer which the system uses in billing document. Open your Manage Business Partner app, check that you have maintained a valid email address for the customer.
  • Make sure that “send by email” is configured for your eDocument process.
    • Practically, you can check in Manage Electronic Documents app, select the eDocument and use the Customer action (this button is visible in your screenshot) to see whether an email recipient can be derived or maintained before you send. See KBA 3251019 for more details.

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: 2602 upgrade period details - when is Q to P transpor...

entry.title: Re: How to restrict unused applications within a catalog
entry.content:
Good day @quocdt5 

Hi

Thank you for your question.

Yes, you can restrict access, but it depends on what you mean by “remove the application”.

The public cloud provides the option to fully remove access by removing the business catalogs that contain the app from the business role. Note that you cannot remove the app if it shares the same catalog with other apps you still need. In this case you will need to decide whether you want to:

  • Keep the catalog or redesign roles using a different catalog set.

SAP provides the possibility for you to separate display and manage apps in different catalogs or organisational and value restrictions that controls the authorisation objects behind the business catalog.

You can practically control this by:

  • Assigning “display” catalog to read only roles
  • Assigning “manage” catalogs to power user roles.

Here is a short guide for your tenant:

Go to your Administration space 

open Identity and Access Management 

Open Maintain Business Roles 

Search for the role, click edit 

Go to Business Catalogs and identify the catalog that contains the app you want to restrict/remove. Remove it from the role and save. 

Optionally, you may also consider removing the tile in your “Spaces and Pages” tab so users do not see it anymore. This is just a visual clean-up.

Let me know if you have further questions.

 

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: WebSocket RFC to Cloud Using SAP Business Connector
entry.content: This blog helped us alot moving forward. In AIF Message Monitoring of SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud however we are still facing “Error when opening an RFC connection (Internal err or ).” but we are missing details as to where this might be originated. Thank you well in advance for your assistance!

entry.title: Mapping custom retention field into the supplier invoice
entry.content:
We are using public cloud.

In Purchase Order, the standard retention field value is not getting printed in the form upon saving.

Therefore we have brought in a BADI and saving the value in Custom CDS from where we are doing the job. But even in that the retention field is getting printed in the form only after editing and making changes to the existing P.O. (Lets say creating a P.O, saving it.. again editing some values which are relevant to print out changes and then saving it again)

Now we have brought in the retention to the custom field. Our primary purpose to print the retention value into the P.O form is resolved.

But we are having difficulties to link the custom field with the supplier invoice line item where system calculates the retention amount (which earlier populated from the standard field in the P.O) for payment block to a special G/L account for a supplier

Currently could not find any badi to make the changes at P.O level.

Any help is much appreciated.

 

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Withholding tax base amount not populating automatica...
entry.content:
Good day @seunabati 

Thank you for your question.

Based on your description and the attached screenshots, there are a two points to be aware of in public cloud:

  1. When your Tax Calculation is set to automatic, it is normal that the WHT Base Amount and WHT Amount fields stay blank on the WHT tab. The reason is, the system calculates them in the background and you must check the posted accounting document to see the calculated base and amount. See KBA 3578497
  2. In your WHT type configuration screenshot, you enabled manual indicators. With those flags, the system tends to shift towards manual entry and can prevent the automatic determination you want.

To enable automatic WHT, in your config:

  1. Correct the Withholding Tax Type control flags in your CBC. Uncheck w/Tax Base Manual, uncheck Manual W/Tax Amount, keep Post WTax Amount as you required and confirm your base amount logic.
  2. In your Definition Withholding Tax Codes, confirm the 2% rate on your screenshot is maintained correctly. Also ensure the base and minimum checks are consistent with your scenario.
  3. In public cloud, you must mark the supplier as subject to WHT and assign the correct WHT type and code in BP tax data, otherwise the system may not apply WHT at invoice and or payment.

You can confirm your WHT was calculated even if the invoice shows blanks after posting the supplier invoice by opening the posted invoice, then navigate to the Journal Entry and check for WHT related lines or WHT info.

If you want WHT at payment, ensure WTax for Payments is set in the WHT type. If not, the system will not withhold at payment as you expect.

A quick guide in your tenant:

  • Open Manage Your solution,
    • search and open “Configure Withholding Tax Types”, “Defining Withholding Tax Codes” and Setting up Withholding Tax for Customer and Supplier Business Partners.
  • Adjust flag, verify code and verify your BP.

You can then retest your scenario by posting a new supplier invoice and check the journal entry. See Defining Withholding Tax Codes and Configure Withholding Tax Types for more details.

 To summarise:

  1. In automatic mode, blank WHT amount fields in the invoice can be normal. Just check your accounting document. See KBA 3447879 for more details.
  2. Your configuration shows you enabled manual WHT, for you to automate, switch them off and enable WTax for payments if you require withholding at payment level.  See Configure Withholding Tax Types and KBA 3578497 for more details.

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Hello experts! is there limitations in public cloud t...
entry.content: Hi, did you figure out if there is a limitation in public cloud regarding the automatic usage decision?

entry.title: Re: Learn SAP Business Transformation - a New Learning Group on SAP Community

entry.title: Customer Self Service Maintenance via SAP for Me of Accounts Payable/Dunning and E-bill Contact
entry.content:
Following the successful rollout of Customer Self Service Maintenance for finance-related contacts earlier last year via SAP for Me, we are pleased to inform you of a further extension of this capability, which went live on December 18th 2025. We are delighted to announce the Go Live of our latest phase of updating the Accounts Payable/Dunning and E-bill Contact(s) within SAP for Me.   

 

From the “Home” page in SAP for Me, you need to go to the “Finance and Legal” page, “Overview” tab.  

You will only see, under the “Your Finance Contacts” card, the contacts for your company that currently hold one or more of the following roles: 

  1. Purchase Order Responsible – Maintenance (PO Resp. MT) 
  2. Purchase Order Responsible – Cloud (PO Resp. Cloud) 
  3. Maintenance Responsible (MT Resp.) the person who should receive the Consumer Price Index (CPI) notification 
  4. Accounts Payable/Dunning Contact 
  5. E-Bill Contact, including: 
  • E-Billing (all Lines of Business (LoB)) 
  • E-Billing Software and Maintenance 
  • E-Billing Cloud  

 

 

You might notice that there are three dots showing at the top right-hand side of the card.  

If you click on this, you will be able to see a more detailed explanation of “What the card is about?” and information of “Required Authorizations”.  

You can filter on each roles, applicable countries and status.  

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Add Role  

Click on the pencil-shaped icon as the edit icon located on the right-hand side of the contact you would like to update. The “Edit Contact” screen will open, where you can view the selected contact’s core information such as first name, last name, and job position, as well as the roles currently assigned. 

In the lower section of this screen, Accounts Payable/Dunning Contact and E-Bill Contact roles can be added by selecting the applicable country from the Applicable Countries options. (E-Billing (all LOB), E-Billing Software & Maintenance, E-Billing Cloud). 

After making the selection, click “Save.” There will be three blue dots that circle whilst the update is in progress. You will now be able to see the contact in the card with the new role assigned. This will also be assigned real time in our back-end systems.    

 

 

 

Create Contact  

If you want to create a contact, click on the “Create” button at the right-hand side of the “Your Finance Contacts”. This brings up a screen where you need to input the mandatory fields – First Name, Last Name, Email, and Job Position, then click “Create Contact”. Mandatory fields are marked with a red asterisk. 

 

A confirmation message will appear indicating that your request is being processed and may take a few minutes. You can continue working during this time. To check the latest status, refresh the card. 

If you enter an e-mail that already exists against a contact in our database, you will receive a warning message that you need to use the existing contact, by adding a role and choosing the e-mail address of the existing contact, rather than creating a duplicate contact.    

Transfer  

Click on the “Transfer” button to the right-hand side of the “Your Finance Contacts”.  

This brings up a screen where you can see the roles you want to transfer. By clicking the downward arrows on the right side, you can view the available options for different LOBs. 

After making your selection, you can search for the contact you want to transfer the role to at the bottom of the screen. Select the contact and then click “Submit”. 

If you would like to transfer a role to a new contact, you must first create the contact. Then, on the role transfer screen, search for the contact using the search field and select it to proceed with the transfer. 

Edit 

This is used for removing or adding a role. You can also edit a contact’s name or e-mail address as long as they are not S user in which case Super Admin will have to edit.    

Click on the pencil-shaped icon as the edit icon. On the screen that opens, you can make the required changes. Click the downward arrow to select the role you want to add or remove, then click “Save”. 

 

Note that you cannot remove a role, unless there is someone else in the company that has the same role.  

Delete  

This is used to delete a contact and break the relationship between the company and the contact.  This will not only remove the contact from “Your Finance Contacts” card but will also remove the contact from the back-end systems.   

There are two caveats to this. There must be someone else with the role assigned, and most importantly, the user must not be an S User.    

Click on the pencil-shaped icon as the edit icon and then “Delete Contact” button.  You will first be asked to ensure that there is another person with that role.  If not, you will need to assign that role to someone else first.  Once you have confirmed “Delete”.  If the user you want to delete is a S User, then you will only be able to remove the role, but the contact will not be deleted in the back-end systems. 

 

 

 

In short, customers can update their own "Purchase Order Responsible Maintenance, Purchase Order Responsible Cloud and Maintenance Responsible, the person who should receive the CPI notification. 

Following the go-live, Accounts Payable/Dunning and E-Bill contacts can also be maintained by customers via self-service. 

Should you have any questions, feel free to reach out to our customer service for assistance.     

Thank you for using “SAP for Me”.

entry.title: Re: How to restrict unused applications within a catalog
entry.content: Thank you so much

entry.title: Re: Sub Contracting With Delivery
entry.content:
Dear all,

is it possible to select the shipping point for the to be created delivery in ADSUBCON or ME2O manually? For us this is not working even if the plant has different shipping points assigned and the determination is setup for this plant, so that the other shipping points are assigned as manual shipping points.

Many thanks!

Regards,

Chris

entry.title: Re: WebSocket RFC – RFC For the Internet
entry.content:
Hi Gregor,

node-rfc is based on the NW RFC Library, so it should already automatically support WebSocket-RFC?! Instead of ASHOST and SYSNR just pass WSHOST and WSPORT in the logon parameter array, and that's it. If you want to do certificate-based logon, replace the user & password parameters with necessary TLS parameters (TLS_CLIENT_PSE etc.) see the documentation in sapnwrfc.ini.

(I hope, the developers of node-rfc were so smart to just pass any logon parameters to the NW RFC library unchanged, then every feature of the NW RFC library is automatically usable without modification on node.js level.)

Not sure about cds-rfc, though. I tried to google a bit, but didn't find the technical details about it. Is it also based on NW RFC library?!

entry.title: Re: In Learning.SAP do we have any courses for SAP Advanc...
entry.content: This is a great news. Thanks for sharing. I will let my peers know about it.

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere Connectivity With S/4 HANA System & SAP Analytics Cloud : Technical Configura
entry.content:
For those asking about Backend roles and privileges -  please consider the following SAP Notes as "Leading" 

 

The following SAP Note explains the roles that should be applied to your SAP Technical User,  this SAP Note explains the procedure SAP Note 2174416 - Creation and activation of Technical Communication Users - SAP for Me  

https://me.sap.com/notes/2174416

The SAP Note below outlines the roles and privileges required - extremely useful SAP Note

SAP Note 3463326 - SAP Datasphere: Metadata Import from SAP S/4HANA On-Premise via "CSN" https://me.sap.com/notes/3463326

The following SAP Note focuses more on the ESH_SERV´CH_SRV which you are going to have to setup for importing CSN entities.

SAP Note 3283282 - Implementation note: Infrastructure for metadata import of CSN entities

https://me.sap.com/notes/3283282

One quick tip when using PFCG to create custom roles,  create a single role like Z_DATASPHERE and copy the required Authorization Objects to it, with the privileges mentioned in SAP Note 3463326 - SAP Datasphere: Metadata Import from SAP S/4HANA On-Premise, 

Hope this helps you all !

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere Connectivity With S/4 HANA System & SAP Analytics Cloud : Technical Configura
entry.content:
@sunildevanda50 

Hi Sunil, good document but needs updating as the screens and UI look has now changed.

Thanks

entry.title: Re: SAP Joule implementation in an S/4HANA private cloud 3 system landscape
entry.content:
@Arunrajj Hi Arunrajj, thank you for sharing your experience. I am suprised though that we need to create system specific catalogs and roles. This not at all in line with SAP best practices for roles and authorizations. Isn't there a way to use a system alias, using a parameter in the respective UI5 Joule plug-in app? Where some how the respective backend S/4 system running the (embedded Fiori LaunchPad) refers to the connected BTP subaccount (running the Joule application)...

Br, Douglas

entry.title: Loss Handling in the Consolidation under Equity method
entry.content:
Dear Experts

We have encountered a scenario in consolidation where one of the entities is accounted for using the equity method, with a shareholding of 35%. Unfortunately, the entity is currently reporting losses, and these losses have exceeded the carrying amount of the investment.

As per IFRS, the investment cannot be written down to zero, and the company does not plan to infuse additional capital. In the subsequent year, once the entity starts generating revenue, these losses will be offset, and the investment will reflect its actual value.

We are currently unable to locate system settings to handle this scenario in consolidation. Could anyone provide guidance or the steps to correctly manage such a situation in the system while remaining IFRS-compliant?

Regards 

Srinivas Thatikonda

entry.title: Re: Withholding tax base amount not populating automatica...
entry.content:
Hi, Please refer to note https://me.sap.com/notes/3383828 to check any missing configuration. 

Thanks Ling

entry.title: Re: Share one number range across 2 systems to facilitate...
entry.content: Hi @Chris1973, could you please help on the following questions raised by MauricevH? Regards, Irene

entry.title: Promotion Creation Reimagined: AI‑Assisted Single & Bulk Workflows in SAP Revenue Growth Management
entry.content:
1. Promotion Creation Friction: Lost Hours and Increased Errors 

For Key Account Managers (KAMs) in the fast-moving consumer goods industry, time is revenue. Yet significant portions of that time are consumed by meticulous, repetitive tasks. Creating a single promotion can be time-consuming — because KAMs need to look up past promotions, select dates and tactics, and manually enter information. That friction not only consumes time but also increases downstream errors in funding, claims, and reconciliation. What if KAMs could reclaim those hours and focus on strategic account growth instead? 

 

2. AI-Assisted Promotion Creation: Embedded in SAP Revenue Growth Management 

To address this challenge, SAP Revenue Growth Management is shipping two embedded AI features: SAP Revenue Growth Management AI‑assisted Trade Promotion Creation (hereinafter “AI-Assisted Single Promotion Creation”) and SAP Revenue Growth Management, AI‑assisted prompt‑based bulk trade promotion creation (hereinafter “AI-Assisted Bulk Promotion Creation”). 

Today, both AI features are embedded directly in the promotion creation workflow, where KAMs need them — empowering teams to work smarter and faster without further navigation. 

For more information on our current capabilities, see An Overview of SAP Revenue Growth Management. 

 

3. Three Ways AI Improves Promotion Creation: Precision, Scale, Control 

3.1. Single Promotion Creation: Name It and Let AI Fill the Rest   

When KAMs want to create a more precise promotion with less effort, they can use AI-Assisted Single Promotion Creation.  

Workflow at a glance

From an Account Plan, KAMs choose Create Promotion and enter a promotion name—for example, "limited Coffee, Christmas promotion, 10% off"—and the AI gets to work. AI offers inline, account‑aware suggestions for promotion details, dates for sell-in and store execution, product groups, trade spends and volume — all of which KAMs can accept or override.  

  • The promotion name serves as the primary cue in Single Promotion Creation because KAMs consistently encode dates, customer and tactic cues in naming conventions — and those cues are powerful, structured inputs for relevant, explainable AI suggestions. 

  • Behind the scenes, the embedded AI will explore the account assortment and product categories within the product hierarchy, identify and disambiguate the possible products inferred in the prompt. Trade Spend recommendations can recognize and utilize whichever trade spends are configured for the account plan. 

 

Significant Time Savings

AI pre-fills most promotion setup fields, including selecting a most appropriate promotion type, setting date ranges for promotion execution and sell-in as well as choosing the spend types, populating requested discount and lump-sum values and making uplift volume recommendations, based on the context of the promotion name and the selected account. Experts estimate this reduces average promotion setup time from around 15 minutes to roughly 3 minutes— a 75% efficiency gain.  

 

Adaptive Learning from User Interactions

The Smart Field Memory in this AI feature learns from KAMs’ manual adjustments. AI‑generated suggestions appear in a pale purple, so they’re easy to spot. When a KAM edits a suggested field and saves, the system logs that override and weight future suggestions for the same account and similar promotion names toward the adjusted value. Over time the AI becomes a personalized assistant—reducing corrections, improving consistency across promotions, and boosting KAM productivity. 

In short, AI-Assisted Single Promotion Creation elevates efficiency by enabling contextual field suggestions and adaptive learning, going beyond simple auto-fill through LLM‑driven contextual understanding of users' intent. 

 

3.2. Bulk Promotion Creation: Enter a Prompt, Generate, and Iterate at Scale 

While single promotion creation handles precise campaigns, KAMs also need to plan multiple promotions quickly to test strategies and ensure portfolio coverage, or to quickly generate a broader set of promotions to test different strategies across their portfolio. For this "speed-to-market" need, KAMs can turn to AI-assisted prompt-based bulk trade promotion creation in SAP Revenue Growth Management.  

Workflow at a glance

From an Account Plan, KAMs choose Create Promotions with AI and enter a natural-language prompt that specifies how many promotions to generate (1–12). KAMs can optionally guide the AI with past promotion data or upload attachments. The AI then generates draft promotions, including promotion details, product groups, trade spends, uplift volume, and provides explainable reasoning detailing how it has interpreted the prompt and what choices it has made in the suggested promotions. KAMs can accept promotions they like or regenerate as needed. When KAMs are mostly satisfied with the generated results, they choose "Save as Drafts and Edit" to refine promotions in full-edit mode before saving them as drafts or choose "Create as Drafts" to keep all generated promotions without further editing. 

 

KAMs can try different types of prompts – for example, KAMs could provide prompts like: 

  • Create 3 summer promotions for limited-edition coffee and coffee capsules with a 25% TPR on-invoice discount and a display for $5000, each promotion should be for 12 days, vary the products promoted in each promotion. 
  • Create a year-long EDLP agreement for dark coffee at 3% 
  • Create 4 promotions for 1 week each in Q4 for the chilled drinks category with an on-invoice discount of 20% and 30% volume uplift 

 

Four Ways to Enhance AI Results

To enter a prompt, KAMs can use natural language or switch to an even more reliable option — Guided Prompt. With Guided Prompt, KAMs fill in or choose values for controlled fields (e.g., number of promotions, promotion type, products, and spend type), and benefit from context engineering, making prompts more precise for tailored results. In addition, KAMs can ground the AI with past promotion data or by uploading attachments. These inputs together provide the AI with the best possible fuel — targeted data and prompts — so it can generate accurate, relevant promotions in seconds. When KAMs start working with a new account or lack historical data, the feature falls back to cold start heuristics business rules (e.g., holiday patterns, common uplift assumptions), so KAMs still get sensible defaults. 

 

Speed and Accuracy Balanced

Bulk Promotion Creation empowers KAMs to rapidly generate many plausible drafts and test tactics at scale without creating each promotion manually. We preserve that balance with a single review step that pairs rapid generation with clear reasoning and edit controls, enabling KAMs to validate suggestions quickly without compromising precision. After generation, KAMs can review all suggested promotions. To check the AI's logic, KAMs click "View Reasoning" to see the prompt interpreted, assumptions made, tasks completed, and any conflicts the AI identified and resolved — helping them accept useful suggestions faster and correct those that don’t fit. 

To sum up, AI-Assisted Bulk Promotion Creation offers a fast‑iteration mode with speed and accuracy, using practical levers to improve results and clear reasoning to support decision-making. 

 

3.3. Continuous Learning with Human-in-the-Loop 

The intelligence within SAP Revenue Growth Management is designed to be dynamic, not static. It evolves from a capable assistant into a trusted partner by learning from every interaction, ensuring suggestions become more accurate and better aligned with business over time, while users retain full authority over the final output. 

  

Self-Learning AI: From Manual Edits to Better Suggestions 

The table below summarizes the key AI enhancement levers available in the two AI features of SAP Revenue Growth Management to drive efficiency and accuracy. 

 

AI Enhancement Levers 

AI-Assisted Single Promotion Creation 

AI-Assisted Bulk Promotion Creation 

Smart Field Memory 

√ 

  

Guided Prompt 

  

√ 

Uploaded Attachments  

 

√ 

Past Promotion Data  

√ 

√ 

Heuristics Business Rules for Cold Start 

√ 

√ 

This table highlights a key synergy: data from past promotions in Bulk Promotion Creation enhances the AI's contextual understanding and can improve suggestions in Single Promotion Creation, forming a cohesive and self-improving system. 

Importantly, these capabilities also reduce downstream errors: consistent, account‑aware suggestions and grounded inputs cut the manual‑entry mistakes that cause funding, claims, and reconciliation headaches. 

  

Human-in-the-Loop Control 

A foundational design principle is that the AI provides suggestions, not commands.  

  • The experience is built around low-risk, high-efficiency interaction. All AI-generated content is clearly identifiable, fully editable, and predictable. KAMs can easily accept, edit, or discard any suggestion, ensuring they're rarely stuck with incorrect pre-filled text that adds manual work.  

  • Each step in promotion creation requires KAMs' explicit confirmation to proceed. This provides a crucial checkpoint, reinforcing trust and ensuring KAMs have the final say on every detail before any promotion is saved. 

In conclusion, this combination of continuous learning, explainable reasoning and human control builds KAMs' confidence in the AI and ensures the AI features truly augments human expertise to drive productivity and accuracy. 

 

4. Co-Innovation on AI-Driven Promotion Excellence 

These AI features are rolling into SAP Revenue Growth Management. They aim to reduce routine work and unlock new levels of efficiency, enabling KAMs to focus on customer strategy rather than form‑filling.  

Whether you're a forward-thinking KAM or a revenue growth leader, if your team is looking to:  

  • explore AI-Assisted promotion creation integrated with Joule 
  • test bulk promotion generation with customers' historical data or attachments 
  • test AI-Assisted deal sheets using a default template or a third-party template 

Get in touch at RGM_Engage@sap.com. Let’s explore a pilot that matches your account workflows and helps solve your most pressing challenges. 

Final Note:  

These new AI features in SAP Revenue Growth Management are being prepared for release and will be generally available soon. 

 

More Information

Documentation on SAP Help Portal

Topic Page for SAP Revenue Growth Management

    • Introducing Calendar View in SAP Revenue Growth Management: A Visual Leap for Strategic Planning
    • Decoding Promotional Success: Embedded Analytics in SAP Revenue Growth Management
    • Unlocking Trade Spend Control Efficiency with Funds Management in SAP Revenue Growth Management
    • Consolidating Customer Planning: Unleash Growth with SAP Revenue Growth Management’s Account Plan
    • Elevate Your Promotion Strategy: Baseline Management with SAP Revenue Growth Management 
    • Driving Promotion Excellence: Streamlined Creation and Copying with SAP Revenue Growth Management
    • Financial Execution & Claims for Trade Promotions with SAP Revenue Growth Management & SAP Cloud ERP
    • Master Your KPIs: How SAP Revenue Growth Management Boosts Data-Driven Growth 
    • Modernize Planning: Universal Hierarchy in SAP Revenue Growth Management for SAP Cloud ERP Customers
    • Promotion Creation Reimagined: AI‑Assisted Single & Bulk Workflows in SAP Revenue Growth Management

entry.title: Der erste Stammtisch Basel - Freiburg
entry.content:
Endlich war es soweit, der erste Stammtisch hat in Basel stattgefunden. In gemütlicher Runde haben wir uns über SAP,  ABAP, Fiori und KI ausgetauscht. Uns kennengelernt und beschlossen, dass auf jeden Fall zu wiederholen 🙂

Der nächste Stammtisch wird in Freiburg stattfinden. Einfach diese Gruppe abonnieren und das Event mitbekommen! Details folgen dann. 

Bastian

entry.title: Re: Clarification on Row Type "NORMAL" behavior
entry.content:
Hi,

Can you share a screenshot to see the generated opcodes?

You would need to execute your job in debug mode and then you can actually see the output of table comparison transform and how it generates opcodes using the binoculars within the dataflow.

I am pretty sure that if no changes between source and target are identified on row level, the rows are omitted/excluded automatically within the table comparison transform. It only lets through actual data changes as Insert/Update/Delete. An unchanged record will be skipped. Kindly check it using the debugger.

So like....Revenue Account is 40000009 then for correction the system should take the same account to book the accruals against. 

Where can we maintain this change in the System? 

 

Thank you in advance. 

BR

Manuel

entry.title: Issue Processing Task 0030 (Balancing of Totals Transactions) in SAP CAR
entry.content:
Hi,

I am facing an issue with task processing in SAP CAR and would appreciate your guidance.

While processing task 0030 (Balancing of Totals Transactions) in the POS Monitoring Workbench, the system returns the error message “Task could not be processed.” However, there is no additional error log or detailed information available to identify the root cause of the failure. Could anyone please help me understand:

What could be the possible reasons for this task failing without detailed error information?

Which logs, traces, or configurations should be checked to further analyze this issue?

Are there any known prerequisites or common issues related to task 0030 that might cause this behavior?

Thanks for the support.

entry.title: Re: how to control a nested cell in a table in adobe form for space control in printing in adobe for
entry.content: Controlling nested cells in Adobe Forms (SAP LiveCycle Designer) for print spacing is best achieved by placing the nested table inside a subform set to "Flowed" and enabling "Allow Page Breaks within Content". To prevent blank space, set the cell/row property to "Hidden" (if excluded from layout) or "Hidden (Exclude from Layout)" via JavaScript or by binding it to an optional field.

entry.title: Tax exclusion for the deposit condition type while posting the Invoice for the PO
entry.content:
Hi @Lakshmipathi sir and SAP Experts,

I have created a new condition type for Deposit in the Purchase Order. The condition is working as expected in the PO, and the deposit amount is posting to a separate G/L account.

As per Lakshmipathi sir’s suggestion, I maintained the condition relevance as Goods/Service Item + Planned Delivery Cost. During invoice posting (MIRO), the deposit amount is coming as a separate line item and is posting successfully.

However, the issue is that tax is also being calculated on the deposit amount.
The business requirement is to calculate tax only on the main material net price, and not on the deposit condition value.

I have attached the configuration screenshots for reference.
Could you please advise if any changes are required in the condition type or pricing procedure so that the deposit condition value is excluded from tax calculation?

Also, please let me know if any general configuration changes are required. I have maintained the configuration based on my understanding, and it is working correctly in the PO, but I would appreciate confirmation or guidance if anything needs to be adjusted.

Your guidance on how to meet this requirement would be highly appreciated.

M/06:

M/08:

entry.title: Re: Asking for CDS View for Free Form Payment App (F2564)
entry.content: Hello @thongle3103, We marked the answer as accepted since there were no further updates on the topic after 7 days. If the answer provided was not helpful enough or you have further questions, you can unaccept it at any time. Best regards, Irene

entry.title: Re: Which SQL tools can use associations of CDS views?
entry.content: Hello, thanks for your answer. In our organization we have a strict separation in the IT team which supports the S/4 Hana (on-premise system 2023FS2, with a target to go to a private cloud). There is a functional team taking care of configuration, process setup, interaction with the business, specifying necessary ABAP developments. And there is a development team that implements what is specified. One task of the functional team is to understand which views of the standard virtual data model can be used to report on the business processes. Are all the used dimensions and measures that we enter in the business process available in a standard view. The CDS views have associations defined which describe the data object better (e.g. for a process order the relation to operations, components; and from operations to work center; and components toproduct information,...). The functional team can use this information to learn more about how data objects are structured and if the association help to get additional information. All this information is then used by the functional team to specify the custom virtual data model, (on top of the standard data model) which describes the master data and transactional data and key figures of our company. The strict separation between functional team and development team blocks the functional team to have S_DEVELOP change/create authority. This is why we look for other tools which can help to understand the existing CDS views. The App "Custom CDS Views" has two versions F1866(Deprecated) and F1866A. You can't create or change views in both if you miss authority for S_DEVELOP. Withouzt S_DEVELOP, the decprecated app F1866 displays released CDS views, the current version F1866A displays only Custom CDS views that are created with this App. Best regards Andreas Ries

entry.title: Re: Can AI Be Effectively Used to Automate Enterprise Wor...
entry.content: AI can be effectively used in enterprise workflows when it augments humans rather than replaces them. Today, AI adds the most value in high-volume, repetitive tasks like document processing, customer support triage, forecasting, and anomaly detection. Human oversight is ensured through human-in-the-loop designs, clear escalation paths, explainable models, and audit logs. Common challenges include data quality, bias, integration with legacy systems, and employee trust. AI should be avoided in contexts requiring ethical judgment, legal accountability, or nuanced human empathy. Balancing automation, accuracy, and accountability requires strong governance, continuous monitoring, and clearly defined ownership of AI-driven decisions.

entry.title: Re: Standard algorithm 001 finding matches outside define...
entry.content:
Hi @kezelda ,

I'm afraid I can't help you completely. However, have you tried to create processing rules for your bank statement processing? Processing rules are the successor feature of the posting rules, and the development team recommends you to switch to processing rules. In processing ruels, you can define conditions,a nd the rule is only activated to process bank statement items if those conditions are fulfilled. You can also select and define various actions that the processing rule should carry out. 

That means, processing rules process yourbank statement items just the way want - if the settings are determined correctly.

For more information, have a look at Processing Rules and How to Create Processing Rules. 

Best regards,
Anita

entry.title: Re: FIN Group Reporting - Breakdown Category
entry.content:
Hello Frank, the breakdown categories are valid for a specific consolidation chart of account. Best practise consolidation chart of account is Y1. Based in your picture you show, that you have create a new one. Therefore you need to create new breakdown categories based on customer requirements.

Birgit

Vigram.

entry.title: Re: Issue in using IntegrationDesigntimeArtifacts API in...
entry.content:
I used the same api to get the configuration of an iFlow.

I used the "Zip Splitter". It decompresses the zip file and splits the result per file.

I also provides a file name pattern so that you can split only the files relevant. (I did struggle a bit with the file name patter, it only worked when It starts with an *, so I ended up using */main/resources/parameters.prop althoug the file shold be located under /src//main/resources/parameters.prop but filtering for this did not work for me ... just telling).

Vigram.

entry.title: Can PR Overall Release enforce per-item approval?
entry.content:
We are using Purchase Requisition – Overall Release workflow in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.
Now each PR item has its own Cost Center and approvers depend on that Cost Center (maintained via custom table).

Example:
Item 10 (CC A --> approvers B/C)
Item 20 (CC D --> approvers E/F)
We need Item 10 to be approved by B or C and Item 20 by E or F before the PR is released. 

Is there any supported way to achieve this with Overall Release, or is Item Release workflow the only option?

br

entry.title: Re: Calculated rating for unofficial user rating
entry.content: We have the same problem. Can we expect a solution here soon?

entry.title: Re: How to fix OOMKilled restart issue with SAP Commerce Cloud
entry.content:
Hello Duc,

Sorry, I've missed your message.

Indeed the feature has been implemented as part of the standard.

We have now a MALLOC_ARENA_MAX = REQUESTED_CPU × MALLOC_ARENA_MAX_RATIO with by default, MALLOC_ARENA_MAX_RATIO is 2. For example,  MALLOC_ARENA_MAX will be 20 for pods with 10 cores.

The ratio is the recommended value but if this is required to set more, Product support engineer can manually set the MALLOC_ARENA_MAX_RATIO environment variable for the relevant services.

Thanks,

Yannick

entry.title: Re: Kyma High availability
entry.content:
Hi @akuller ,

Please find details below:

Kyma is highly available inside a region. This means that the 3 nodes gets deployed on 3 different availability zones inside a region.  https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-business-technology-platform/availability-zones-in-kyma-environment 

Rearding the components, e.g. the Kyma modules are getting updated on a regular basis. They are zero downtime updates.

The major updates (e.g. Kubernetes version upgrade) follow the BTP maintenance window schedule. https://support.sap.com/en/my-support/systems-installations/cac/maintenance-windows.html?anchorId=section_1497935568

Ensuring that your applications are also highly available, you can set up multiple replicas across different availability zones. https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-business-technology-platform/resilient-applications-in-kyma-environment 

BR

Gaurav

entry.title: Re: FIN Group Reporting - Breakdown Category
entry.content:
I can add that you will see that when you create a new chart of account most configuration need to be re-created/adopted to your requirements.

In SAP note 2659672 - FAQ About SAP S/4HANA Finance for Group Reporting (On Premise) there are attached a document 'how to create new new COA and Replaces FS Items_1909.pdf'. This will properly still be valid for your case guiding you through the relevant steps to be performed.

Br

Martin Jul

Regards,

Zenon

entry.title: Compatibility Pack: How to check it in your system going forward?
entry.content:
Hello, and thanks for spending some time reading this blog post! 

Today, I want to address Compatibility Package (CP) use rights issue and how to check them in your system going forward. To know more about compatibility packs, compatibility scope matrix and way forward details please refer note 2269324.

As you might be aware, the compatibility use rights for On-Prem S/4HANA customers were extended till May 31st, 2026. After this period, customers who do not operate under a cloud subscription are not allowed to use CP functionalities anymore.

Now, what changes are expected with this new extension?

There is going to be one source of information for customers to check CP usage in their systems and it is your Simplification Item check! To know more details about this, please refer to note 2399707.

We’ve modernized our different compatibility pack usage reports and combined them in the simplification item check (Please implement the latest version of note 2399707 before proceeding further!)! Run the report /SDF/RC_START_CHECK, select the radio button “New relevance check for Compatibility Packages” and execute. Here, please note that report runs for the current SP of the system in which you would want to check the CP usage, the current SP version is selected by default once you choose radio button to check CP usage (Always make sure to run it in the system where you would want to check the CP usage, it is client dependent!).

 

 

 

Once the report is executed, you could see the list of CP items used in your system. If you would like to know their usage, then please go to application logs after the execution of report (Please check the log only after you have executed the report). Here, you could see the usage data item wise in your list. It will give you item usage data in the last 3 months (3M) and last 1 month (1M). It will also show the item as irrelevant if in the last 12 months usage was 0 (object was used 0 times in period of 12 months).

 

 

 

Once you have the data from execution of report, you could see the alternatives for those items from your way forward excel maintained in note 2269324. SAP recommends you move to alternate solutions before the expiry of your use rights!  Please reach out to your local SAP account representative to discuss your options.

For Customers operating their S/4HANA system under cloud subscription there is no change in usage rights, it is extended till end of December 2030 as communicated before!

For Customers who are not currently under the maintenance contract with SAP, Please reach out to SAP!

#Compatibility Packs                #Compatibility Usage                #Compatibility Use Rights              SAP S/4HANA         #SAP S/4HANA On-Prem

entry.title: Re: Backflush program based on Plant with Sequential Sorting per Order- Enhanced Feature from S4H 20
entry.content:
Hi Shailesh,

Nice blog.

The lock is not plant or order-based, still. Plant-based job schedules can block each other. It would be nice if SAP also added this functionality.

BR

Lokman

entry.title: VAT Codes clarification BE & ES
entry.content:
Hi, 

We'r implementing RITA in one of our company codes running S4 public cloud, a company situated in Netherlands with plant abroad in Belgium and Spain. We've activated RITA and set to status 5 in all of our systems, so we'r now ready to preform the configuration activity Defining Tax Accounts to add the dimension tax country. We've therefor with a help of a tax expert defined which tax-codes should be added into the the configuration, since we're expecting small businesses in the tax countries we'd like to add only a few. However, the tax expert has some questions I'll need some support with related to tax codes in BE and ES, please find the questions below: 

  1. BE: Which tax code should be used for the domestic reverse charge. Sales of goods and services in Belgium by a non-established entity to an established taxable person in Belgium is subject to a domestic (local) reverse charge. This would likely be applicable if JISU (company code NL06) imports vehicles and sells the vehicles within Belgium (B2B)?
  2. BE: What does tax code 99 (Sale BE Exempt VAT-Unit) refer to. Is that meant to be used in case we would have local exempt sales?  
  3. ES: What should tax code “AF” be used to (“Non subject due localization rules – Services”)?

Kind Regards, Sanne

entry.title: Re: Tax exclusion for the deposit condition type while po...
entry.content:
• If MWST is set to calculate tax from PBXX (material price) up to a line number that includes your deposit condition, you need to change the “To” so it stops before the deposit condition line.
  • Example:
    • PBXX = line 100
    • Deposit = line 200
    • MWST currently “From 100 To 200” → includes deposit.
    • Change MWST to “From 100 To 100” → only material net price is taxed.
  • Alternative approach is create a separate subtotal (e.g., “Net price before deposit”) and point MWST to that subtotal. This way, tax is always calculated only on the material net price, regardless of other conditions.

entry.title: In SAC, how to setup aggregation type 'None' for measure/account dimensions to avoid auto summing
entry.content:
I have a model were we are loading all level data into the model. i.e leaf and parents level data is loaded from file into model. While reporting I want to disable the default aggregation type - SUM. So I have given 'None' for all the account members. Still my data is getting aggregated and added to the already loaded data at parent level and doubling the value.

How do we avoid this and perform a straight forward store and retrieve reporting.

Also tried to set aggregation type to 'None' in the measure settings, but could not see 'None' option available.

entry.title: Re: FIN Group Reporting - Breakdown Category
entry.content: Thank you so much - both of you!

entry.title: New ESAC (CQC) Pilot for Customers: Deployment Readiness Check for the Integrated Toolchain
entry.content:
Greetings SAP Community!

We are excited to announce a new Continuous Quality Check (CQC) Deployment Readiness Check for the Integrated Toolchain, available as a pilot offering through the SAP Enterprise Support Advisory Council (ESAC) beginning in Q1 2026.

This new CQC engagement supports customers in validating the technical foundation required to integrate SAP Signavio, SAP Cloud ALM, and SAP LeanIX into a unified, interoperable toolchain. The goal is simple: help ensure your environment is ready for seamless end‑to‑end process transformation support.

The Deployment Readiness Check for the Integrated Toolchain focuses on verifying that your technical setup supports:

  • Consistent data flow across SAP Signavio, SAP Cloud ALM, and SAP LeanIX
  • Properly established foundational configurations
  • A unified and efficient experience aligned with SAP best practices
  • Safe and confident progression toward go‑live

This CQC provides a remote assessment of key configuration elements needed to integrate these solutions and ensures your setup is prepared for seamless interoperability across the toolchain.

During the CQC engagement, SAP experts will:

  • Review the core configuration prerequisites across SAP Signavio, SAP Cloud ALM, and SAP LeanIX
  • Validate whether your setup supports a consistent and efficient toolchain
  • Outline deployment‑readiness observations and identify any integration risks
  • Provide best‑practice recommendations to strengthen your configuration
  • Summarize findings and deliver an action plan in a final report

This engagement helps safeguard your project, ensuring your technical landscape is ready before transformative activities begin.

To participate in this pilot CQC, customers should have:

  • SAP LeanIX, SAP Signavio, and SAP Cloud ALM solutions licensed
  • Administrator permissions in the relevant systems
  • A technical contact available during the engagement
  • An environment already provisioned for initial technical checks

These prerequisites ensure a smooth review and allow the SAP team to provide actionable insights based on your live configuration.

As part of the engagement, you will receive:

  • A remote analysis performed by SAP experts
  • A detailed report outlining the findings
  • Clear recommendations and action items for your configuration

entry.title: Re: default issuing storage location in purchase requisit...
entry.content: What is maintained in issuing storage location? For information, Receiving SLoc comes from MRP 2 view. Issuing SLoc comes from special procurement key customizing. If you leave the issuing SLoc blank in customizing, MRP cannot fill it in the PR.

entry.title: Re: Z* Custom CDS Views Not Visible in SAC Live Connectio...
entry.content:
Hi,

You also need to set CDS API state to C1-Released; its likely to be the missing bit (2595552 - Cannot access Core Data Services (CDS) View from SAP S/4HANA in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC)).

Kind Regards

entry.title: Re: How We Built a Smart Data Scrambling Solution for SAP SuccessFactors HCM Using SAP BTP
entry.content:
Thank you for your interest! Yes, the Data Scrambling Solution for SAP SuccessFactors HCM is available for customers. We’d be happy to discuss how the solution works, deployment options, and licensing details.

For additional information, please write to us at kavya.manohara@techenvo.com and we’ll get back to you with the next steps.

@MatthiasL

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud Integration/CPI: How to Return Custom Error Messages to Sender - available options
entry.content:
Hi @zeal_it_christopher_linke, In addition to the standard Status/Status Group parameters, the message exception monitoring filter now includes a 'Custom Status' field. Let me know if this works for you.

br

Mateusz

entry.title: Re: Revenue Sharing Across Business Units from Main Contract in SAP BRIM: CC or CI?
entry.content: Can anyone provide pointers for Option 2. It seems a fairly straight forward requirement but in BRIM it appears it is not so straightforward to implement.

entry.title: Re: Clarification on Row Type "NORMAL" behavior
entry.content:
Not a problem:

If you have actual data changes you can also check in the debugger.

This blog shows you how you can see the opcodes in the debugger: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-members/table-comparison-transform-sap-bods/ba-p/13558926

entry.title: Re: Customer Self Service Maintenance via SAP for Me of Accounts Payable/Dunning and E-bill Contact
entry.content:
Hello,,

In the "Finance contacts" app/tile in SAP for me, more people appear that the company I work for has S users for (active and deleted combined).  How is that so?

What users should show in the "Finance contacts" app/tile?

I understand I could need to raise this somewhere else.  If so, can you guide a bit more than "customer service"?

Best Regards, Wallace

entry.title: Re: Manager View in MSTeams SuccessFactors App
entry.content:
Thanks for your feedback @LindaS So far we implemented bunch of notifications based on the SF Homepage approvals and for your today engagement cards, see the full list here - https://help.sap.com/docs/successfactors-platform/integrating-sap-successfactors-with-microsoft-365/sap-successfactors-notification-cards-in-microsoft-teams . This also include some notifications for manager persona. Hope that helps.

For manager view, we are currently waiting and aligning the manager experience strategy with other modules in SuccessFactors. Any ideas from your side are welcome as always!

For "Assisted Microsoft Teams App Chat", you can use natual language to trigger our pre-defined quick action list so that end users can efficiently finish their tasks in the app.

entry.title: Re: Data Migration Cockpit - VC Material variant
entry.content: Hi, Did you try this SAP Help Link https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/d3a3eb7caa1842858bf0372e17ad3909/f24b02e065fe48ff847cc9e1d4145e30.html?locale=en-US this talks about the VC - Material variant migration !!

entry.title: Re: SAP DMC Batch transfer from ERP
entry.content: Hi! If my answer was helpful, please consider accepting it as solution.

entry.title: How to display text beside value in organizational assignment transaction PA30 ?
entry.content:
Hallo expert, 

I have a case in transaction code PA30 on the organizational assignment tab in the enterprise structure section.
Some text values, such as the company code, are not appearing, and neither are others.
How can I get this text to appear?
Is this a configuration or enhancement program?

Thank you for your attention.
Best Regards

entry.title: Is SAP still looking for its identity?
entry.content:
I've been around SAP since 2004. And lately, I think I begun to understand what's SAP's problem. And I guess the problem is that SAP has not found its identity (yet?).

If SAP was a person, we'd say SAP has ADHD: it's unable to keep focus and changes direction and even behaviour every other day.

SAP is unable to stick around a single concept, architecture, product, even image except the SAP blue logo.

I dunno if it's because its parents are away, because it's grown too much and expanded too far, or if it's because it has too many people trying to justify their salaries.

As it's not my money, I really don't care, except because it made me lose any trust I could have on SAP ever.

Every new SAP announcement flows through my brain like sugar: a small activity peak and then a sense of emptyness.

Maybe it's just me. But I still wonder if some day SAP will find its identity and will be able to stick with it, making my professional life a bit easier.

entry.title: Re: Is SAP still looking for its identity?
entry.content: I've been forced to ad the "Channel 1" label, although I don't know even what does it mean.

entry.title: Re: there is no linkage between sales order and STO PO nu...
entry.content:
Good day @lithika_nandhakumar 

Thank you for your question.

In public cloud, yes, there is no single standard CDS view (released) that gives you the process flow you described in one dataset. The public cloud only offers these CDS content specifically for objects e.g., SO, PR, PO etc. See https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/c0c54048d35849128be8e872df5bea6d/8573b810511948c8a99c0672abc159aa.html for more details.

You can trace the chain E2E through MRP element and pegging views in the Material Coverage apps.

I suggest you:

  • Use Material Coverage and export because it is a standard solution in public cloud if your requirement is to trace and report without building custom CDS joins.

Here is a quick guide:

  • In your tenant, open Monitor Material Coverage – Net and Individual Segments. Filter by your material, plant and segment type. 

  • Open the material and check coverage. Depending on your setup, you will see requirement elements and matching receipts elements e.g., Planned Order, PR and STO. Then you can export and use the data to build your demand tracking report outside the app.

Consult these references for more insights:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/2bba750d1e124e1ea2a039bb1cd9b6c5/1ab27d576b8aab76e10000000a441470.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/2bba750d1e124e1ea2a039bb1cd9b6c5/7859921c79da489daf568710762da978.html

Let me know if you have further questions.

 

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Discover the right SAP solutions — fast.
entry.content:
SAP Solution Scout helps you cut through complexity and find SAP products that match your goals. Instead of digging through documentation, you get a focused, easy-to-navigate view of the SAP portfolio with clear guidance on capabilities and benefits.

What it does

  • Curates solutions for your industry (retail, life sciences, industrial manufacturing, etc.) so you see only relevant options including industry specifics
  • Highlights technical specs and business impact to help you compare choices quickly
  • Uses a modern, user-friendly interface that speeds decision-making

What’s new

  • Powered by the SAP Reference Architecture: Matches are anchored in a consistent business and solution architecture, giving customers, sellers and partners a common language to assess fit and build a clear joint value story.

Why it matters

  • Save time by avoiding irrelevant options
  • Choose solutions that meet your future needs
  • Gain industry-specific insights to stay competitive

Start exploring with SAP Solution Scout and make smarter, faster decisions that drive efficiency and growth.

entry.title: Discover the right SAP solutions—fast.
entry.content:
SAP Solution Scout helps you cut through the complexity and find SAP products that match your goals. Instead of digging through documentation, you get a focused, easy-to-navigate view of the SAP portfolio with clear guidance on capabilities and benefits.

What it does

  • Curates solutions for your industry (retail, manufacturing, healthcare, etc.) so you see only relevant options.
  • Highlights technical specs and business impact to help you compare choices quickly.
  • Uses a modern, user-friendly interface that speeds decision-making.

What’s new

  • Powered by the SAP Reference Architecture: Matches are anchored in a consistent business and solution architecture, giving sellers and partners a common language to assess fit and build a clear joint value story.

Why it matters

  • Save time by avoiding irrelevant options.
  • Choose solutions that scale with your future needs.
  • Gain industry-specific insights to stay competitive.

Start exploring with SAP Solution Scout and make smarter, faster decisions that drive efficiency and growth.

entry.title: Re: Some issues with the SuccessFactors Microsoft Teams i...
entry.content: @Ale_Comsa, all business data listed here can be consumed by SuccessFactors side customization and translation. - https://help.sap.com/docs/successfactors-platform/integrating-sap-successfactors-with-microsoft-365/business-data-sources So for CPM - req feedback / give feedback, the default questions can be aligned.

entry.title: Re: SAP Sustainability Control Tower-Import Greenhouse Gas Emissions from SAP EHS Environment Manage
entry.content:
Hi @Pardhasaradhi_Reddy_Chelikam ,
Could you please say from where can we obtain Scope 3 data for the production process? For example, how can we get data for S3.1 – Purchased Goods and Services (GHG Scope Category 3.1) from S/4 Hana?

entry.title: Re: Some issues with the SuccessFactors Microsoft Teams integration
entry.content: @Ale_Comsa, all business data listed here can be consumed by SuccessFactors side customization and translation. - https://help.sap.com/docs/successfactors-platform/integrating-sap-successfactors-with-microsoft-365/... So for CPM - req feedback / give feedback, the default questions can be aligned.

entry.title: Re: Some issues with the SuccessFactors Microsoft Teams i...
entry.content: @jsysel, are you saying you expect payslips to be in the list as well? Our quick action list is based on the SuccessFactors Homepage setting and also our own feature switch and also the permission setting as a combined logic to determine display or not, see details here. https://help.sap.com/docs/successfactors-platform/integrating-sap-successfactors-with-microsoft-365/sap-successfactors-quick-actions-in-microsoft-teams. If you still have problem, you can raise cases to us. Thanks for the feedback!
